Chapter 1 | GOOD STRATEGY IS UNEXPECTED|
Q&A

What is the significance of a good strategy being
'unexpected'?

How did Steve Jobs manage to turn Apple around when
most did not expect success?

What was the 'left hook' strategy in the Gulf War, and
why was it surprising?

Why do complex organizations fail to implement focused
strategies?

How did Sam Walton break conventional wisdom with
Wal-Mart's strategy?
What's an example of 'fluff' from the text?

Why is it critical to face challenges head-on in strategy
formulation?

What underlying issue did the Author point out about the
LAUSD's approach to improving schools?

What should effective strategic planning focus on instead
of vague aspirations?

Can you provide an analogy to clarify the importance of
actionable strategies?

What ultimate lesson can be drawn about crafting
strategies?

Chapter 4 | WHY SO MUCH BAD STRATEGY?|
Q&A
What determines the effectiveness of a strategy?

What impact does the inability to make choices have on
organizational strategy?

Why does the text criticize the New Thought approach to
strategy?

In what way did Eisenhower's strategy differ from mere
visionary leadership?

Chapter 5 | THE KERNEL OF GOOD STRATEGY|
Q&A

What is the kernel of a good strategy according to
Rumelt?
How can a diagnosis transform strategic options?

What lesson about organizational change can be gleaned
from the case of Lou Gerstner at IBM?

In what way does coherence in action contribute to
strategic advantage?

What does Rumelt imply about the relationship between
organization and strategy?

How does Rumelt use the example of World War II to
illustrate strategic coordination?

What is the ultimate takeaway regarding good strategy
from Chapter 5?
What defines a 'pivot point' in strategic planning?

How does the concept of concentration enhance strategic
effectiveness?

Can you illustrate the consequences of failing to
anticipate strategic outcomes?

Why is the Getty Trust's strategy under Harold Williams
significant?

Why is resolving ambiguity critical in setting proximate
objectives?

How can organizations avoid getting stuck in
low-effectiveness states?

Why is it challenging for competitors to replicate IKEA's
model?

What role does identifying limiting factors play in
strategic investment?

Why is it necessary to view strategy as more than just
decision-making?

How does the example of BMW illustrate the concept of
design in strategy?

What danger does reliance on strategic resources pose, as
seen in the case of Xerox?

World' best ideas
unlock your potential

What drives successful growth within a company rather
than just size?

How did Avery's approach to growth at Crown Cork &
Seal ultimately lead to challenges?

What did Avery overlook in his chase for growth in the
PET industry?

What should companies focus on to achieve sustainable
growth?

What illustrates the dangers of pursuing growth without
a clear focus?

How can companies avoid the pitfalls of growth through
acquisition?

What was the outcome for Crown Cork & Seal after
Avery's strategy of acquisition?

What does the term 'economies of mass' mean in the
context of corporate mergers?

dynamic nature in business, using examples from Stewart
Resnick's companies.

Why is 'interesting' advantage significant for a business's
long-term success?

How do deepening and broadening advantages contribute
to a business's growth?

What are the pitfalls of assuming that competitive
advantage will automatically lead to wealth?

Why is strengthening isolating mechanisms important for
maintaining a competitive edge?

What is the significance of holding the 'high ground' in
strategy?

Why is having historical perspective crucial during times
of change?

What's the importance of discerning the 'second-order
changes' after a major event?

What differentiates Cisco Systems' success in the
telecommunications industry?

How did inertia play a role in the downfall of Blockbuster
against Netflix?
What are the three categories of organizational inertia?

What lessons can be drawn from the example of AT&T's
cultural inertia?
How can organizations combat entropy effectively?

What is the significance of 'inertia by proxy' in business
dynamics?

How did General Motors exemplify entropy and inertia
leading up to its 2008 bankruptcy?

Why is the constant management of entropy critical for
organizational success?

How did Nvidia manage its relationships with board
makers?

for graphics performance?

What mistakes did Nvidia's competitors make that

What obstacles did Nvidia face in maintaining its lead in
